introduction.html.title: Introduction
introduction.html.description:
Campaign Overview A death curse plaguing all of Toril emanates from the jungle island of Chult. Ancient ruins and hordes of undead fill the mostly unexplored and dangerous jungles. The mercantile city of Port Nyanzaru stands as a bastion of civilization on the northern edge of the jungle. The tone of the game is fantasy Indiana Jones / The Mummy. Overall the jungle and especially the tomb are very dangerous and unforgiving; though some encounters will be light and even slightly 'wacky' fun.
The jungles of Chult are largely unexplored and very dangerous, filled with dinosaurs, strange goblins, and a lot of undead. Wilderness survival is a key element of much of the game.
The peninsula's only city is Port Nyanzaru, a bustling port built on trade, filled with unique wonders, and run by powerful and enigmatic Merchant Princes. Character Creation: The Death Curse (in part) affects people who have been previously raised from the dead (They started wasting away). If you like, your character can have folks in their life who have been raised from the dead before the adventure starts; this might help motivate them to pursue the main external goal of the story (but that's not required!). The Death Curse started about 20 days before the start of the adventure and is widely talked about.
Characters can be from Chult, from the Sword Coast, or from anywhere. The current start idea is that you are all adventurer’s with a benefactor in Baldur’s Gate (I might change this based on your characters)
The game will probably begin in Baldur's Gate unless all / most of you decide your characters are from Chult.
Native Races from Chult are: Humans, Dwarves, Tabaxi and Aarakocra (Let me know if you want more details) The Campaign is supposed to be difficult, so be as Meta in your character creation as you want, feel free to work together to synergize characters and mechanics All Dnd 5.5e content published by WOTC is fair game (if it was reprinted for 2024, you have to use the new stuff 😉). Any 3rd party / Homebrew just run it by me. Characters start at level 2, and get the basic starting equipment or the gold equivalent. You may use point buy for your stats, or roll for your stats (4d6dl 6 times to create an array.) Mechanics to Keep in Mind: Revive magic doesn't work anymore. Traveling
On the map of Chult, each hex measures 10 miles across. Characters moving at a normal pace can travel 1 hex per day on foot through coastal, jungle, mountain, swamp, or wasteland terrain. They can travel 2 hexes per day if they're traveling by canoe on a river or lake. If characters move at a fast pace, they have a 50% chance to advance 1 additional hex that day. Characters moving at a fast pace take a penalty to their passive Wisdom (Perception) scores. If characters set a slow pace, they have a 50% chance to advance 1 fewer hex that day. Characters moving at a slow pace can move stealthily. As long as they're not in the open, they can try to surprise or sneak by other creatures they encounter. Dehydration At the end of each day, any character who hasn't drunk at least 2 gallons of fresh water must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or suffer 1 level of exhaustion. The saving throw is made with disadvantage if the character is wearing medium armor, heavy armor, or heavy clothing. Characters traveling at a fast pace, instead of a normal or slow pace, take a penalty on their saving throws against dehydration. A long rest will not restore exhaustion until the character consumes enough fresh water. Weather
Chult is hot, humid, and rainy throughout the year. The temperature regularly climbs as high as 35 degrees C during the day and seldom falls below 20 degrees C even at night. A day without rain is rare, but rain varies from a steady mist to drenching downpours. Visibility in heavy rain is limited to 50 yards. Beyond that distance, only Huge or larger objects can be distinguished. Missile weapon ranges are halved during rain.

locations/chult.html.title: Chult
locations/chult.html.description:
Location and Composition: Chult is a peninsula ringed with mountains and choked with rainforests. It is described as a tropical wilderness. The interior consists mostly of jungles, plateaus, impassable mountains, and belching volcanoes. Climate: The region is consistently hot, humid, and rainy throughout the year. A day without rain is rare Navigation Challenges: Mapping the peninsula has historically been nearly impossible. Rivers are often sluggish, making it difficult to discern upstream from downstream. Perils of the Wilds: The interior jungle is prowled by enormous reptiles (dinosaurs) and an army of undead. The only major settlement and safest entry point into Chult is Port Nyanzaru, located on the coast at the south end of the Bay of Chult.The party is aware of various local groups and pervasive dangers: Undead: The jungle is overrun by a blight of undead creatures. Chultans: The native human population is known as Chultans.
Other Races: Members of every race can be found in Port Nyanzaru.

headers.heading: Civilization: Port Nyanzaru

locations/port-nyanzaru.html.title: Port Nyanzaru
locations/port-nyanzaru.html.description: Atmosphere and Appearance: The city is bustling, colorful, and noisy. Buildings are painted in bright shades of blue, green, orange, and salmon pink.Governance: The city is ruled by seven Merchant Princes, who control monopolies on all merchandise sold within the city.Economy and Hazards: The city is surrounded by enemies, including ferocious reptiles and murderous undead. Dinosaurs are commonly used as beasts of burden to haul carts and tow boats along canals. Ekene-Afa Ifan Talro'a Jessamine Jobal (Guides and Sellswords) Kwayothé Wakanga O'tamu (Magic and knowledge) Zhanthi The Red Bazaar: The Red Bazaar is where locals shop for everyday needs such as meat, vegetables, fruit, insect repellent, and rain catchers. The Thundering Lizard (Inn): It is known that wilderness guides frequent this establishment looking for work. Kaya's House of Repose (Inn): It is known to be much quieter than the Thundering Lizard. The Grand Souk: Traders from up and down the Sword Coast come to buy timber, spices, medicines, dinosaur parts, ivory, and other riches of Chult here. The Lighthouse: A flame burns atop this structure day and night to guide ships through darkness, fog, and rain into the harbor Fort Nyanzaru: This fort contains a winch for raising and lowering an immense iron chain stretched between the fort and the lighthouse. When the chain is raised, it prevents any ship from sailing into or out of the harbor. The fort also mounts two ballistas. The Warehouse District: This area consists of warehouses used to store goods being shipped into or out of Port Nyanzaru. Canals are cut between the rows of warehouses, allowing ships to be towed directly to the desired warehouse by harnessed dinosaurs for easy loading and unloading.

lore/soulmonger.html.title: Soulmonger
lore/soulmonger.html.description:
The Soulmonger is a necromantic artifact. It is the traced source of the worldwide "death curse".The function of the Soulmonger is directly tied to the two main aspects of the curse: Wasting Affliction: It is draining the life force from individuals who have been previously raised from the dead. For instance, the party's benefactor, Syndra Silvane. Preventing Resurrection: It is preventing future resurrections.
Intelligence Source: Information about the artifact was gathered by Syndra Silvane through her contacts, which included the Harpers. The Harpers determined the cause of the death curse is this device. General Location: The Soulmonger is believed to be hidden on the peninsula of Chult. Mission Goal: The characters have been charged with traveling to Chult to locate and neutralize the Soulmonger.

npcs/syndra-silvane.html.title: Syndra Silvane
npcs/syndra-silvane.html.description:
The characters know the following about Syndra Silvane: Role and Status: She is the party's benefactor, a retired adventurer, and a merchant. The Death Curse: Syndra revealed that she is afflicted by the worldwide "death curse" because she was raised from the dead years ago. This affliction causes her to wither and die, a process that conventional clerics cannot stop. Past Experience: She mentioned that she had died once and been raised from the dead, and has since closed the door on her adventuring life. She also confirmed that she had already sent other, more experienced parties on this quest when pressed by the adventurers Syndra Silvane laid out the following information regarding the expedition: The Source of the Curse: Her contacts, including the Harpers, determined that the death curse originates from a necromantic artifact called the Soulmonger.
The Objective: She tasked the adventurers with traveling to the peninsula of Chult to locate and neutralize the Soulmonger before the curse claims her life. Reward: She promised each character one rare and one uncommon magic item upon the successful completion of the quest.
Travel and Support: To expedite their journey, Syndra used a teleport spell to transport the group directly to Port Nyanzaru, the only major settlement in Chult. She provided the party with an incomplete map of Chult. Post-Arrival Plans: Upon arrival, Syndra departed to stay with her friend, Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu. She recommended securing lodging at either the Thundering Lizard or Kaya's House of Repose. She informed the characters that Wakanga O'tamu is one of the seven merchant princes who rule the city.

sessions/session-1.html.title: Session 1
sessions/session-1.html.description:
The first session of the Tomb of Annihilation module began with the presentation of the worldwide "death curse". This affliction targets everyone who has ever been raised from the dead, causing them to wither and die, and preventing future resurrections.The player characters—including Mistral Honeygleam (a cheerful, small, level 1 caster), and Sir Caldryn Keyne (an arrogant, well-dressed, wealthy High Elf/Sun Elf paladin)—were gathered at a house in Baldur's Gate. They were acquainted and had worked together previously.The party's benefactor, Syndra Silvane, revealed she was afflicted by the curse because she had been raised from the dead years ago. She consulted with contacts, including the Harpers, who traced the source of the curse to a necromantic artifact called the Soulmonger. The artifact is believed to be hidden within the ruins of an older civilization deep within Chult.
Syndra Silvane charged the adventurers with traveling to the peninsula of Chult to locate and neutralize the Soulmonger before the curse fully drained her life force. As a reward, she promised each character one rare and one uncommon magic item upon successful completion of the quest.
To expedite travel, Syndra Silvane intended to use a teleport spell to transport the party to the only major settlement in the region, Port Nyanzaru. The characters agreed, though Tidus Thaloren initially preferred to travel by sea.
Upon arrival, the party found themselves in the bustling, colorful, and noisy tropical city of Port Nyanzaru. Syndra Silvane departed to stay with her friend, Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu. She recommended the adventurers secure lodging at either the Thundering Lizard (for a "raucous time") or Kaya's House of Repose (for a "good night's sleep"), both located near the Red Bazaar.Sir Caldryn Keyne resolved to seek lodging at the Thundering Lizard for revelry. Mistral Honeygleam spoke to the harbormaster (described as a large half gold dragon, half man) for directions to the Red Bazaar.While in the city, the party began investigating locations and seeking guidance: Wakanga O'tamu: Sir Caldryn Keyne determined that the Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu dealt primarily in magic items and kept knowledge/information in scrolls. Guide and Side Quest: While at the inn, a middle-aged woman approached the party, offering her services as a guide. She asked if they were heading toward Mbala. She revealed that she was seeking to eradicate the evil dwelling in the ghost town of Mbala, specifically the hag known as Nanny Pu'pu. The guide offered her services for free if the party agreed to help her destroy this evil. The party accepted this arrangement. The guide agreed to meet them at sunrise the following morning, confirming that she would handle guiding and navigation but the party was responsible for their food and water. The party purchased essential gear, including insect repellent balm and incense and rain catchers, and planned to start their expedition the next day.

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
